markdown.headingText: 2. List in order of publication date (Newest first)
markdown.markdown:
**Publications and Preprints**
------------------------------------------------------------------------ ```{r,echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=T, results='asis'} library(dplyr) library(rcrossref) library(readxl) library(tidyverse) pubs <- read_xlsx("data/publications.xlsx") citation <- cr_cn(dois = pubs$doi, format = "text", style = "plant-physiology") my_vector <- unlist(citation) pubs <- pubs %>% mutate(fullref = str_sub(my_vector,1,-1)) pubs <- pubs %>% arrange(desc(year)) for (k in sort(unique(pubs$year), decreasing = TRUE)) { pubs.year <- pubs %>% filter(year == k) cat(paste0("#### **", k, "** \n\n")) # Added extra newline for MD spacing for(i in 1:nrow(pubs.year)) { current_ref <- pubs.year$fullref[i] ## Change the Jo L with bold_ref <- ifelse(pubs.year$correspondingauthor[i] == "yes" & pubs.year$cofirstauthor[i] == "no",str_replace(current_ref,"Jo L", "Jo L†"),ifelse( pubs.year$correspondingauthor[i] == "no" & pubs.year$cofirstauthor[i] == "yes",str_replace(current_ref,"Jo L", "Jo L§"),ifelse( pubs.year$correspondingauthor[i] == "yes" & pubs.year$cofirstauthor[i] == "yes",str_replace(current_ref,"Jo L", "Jo L†,§"), str_replace(current_ref,"Jo L", "Jo L")))) corr_label <- if(pubs.year$correspondingauthor[i] == "yes") " (†Corresponding Author)" else "" coauthor_label <- if(pubs.year$cofirstauthor[i] == "yes") " (§Co-first author)" else "" doi.link <- paste0( '', 'http://doi.org/', pubs.year$doi[i], '' ) # 4. Print the formatted lines cat(str_glue('
  • {bold_ref} {doi.link}{corr_label}{coauthor_label}

')) } } ```

**Publications and Preprints**
------------------------------------------------------------------------ ```{r,echo=FALSE, message=FALSE, warning=T, results='asis'} library(dplyr) library(rcrossref) library(readxl) library(tidyverse) pubs <- read_xlsx("data/publications.xlsx") citation <- cr_cn(dois = pubs$doi, format = "text", style = "plant-physiology") my_vector <- unlist(citation) pubs <- pubs %>% mutate(fullref = str_sub(my_vector,1,-1)) # 2. List in order of publication date (Newest first) pubs <- pubs %>% arrange(desc(year)) for (k in sort(unique(pubs$year), decreasing = TRUE)) { pubs.year <- pubs %>% filter(year == k) cat(paste0("#### **", k, "** \n\n")) # Added extra newline for MD spacing for(i in 1:nrow(pubs.year)) { current_ref <- pubs.year$fullref[i] ## Change the Jo L with bold_ref <- ifelse(pubs.year$correspondingauthor[i] == "yes" & pubs.year$cofirstauthor[i] == "no",str_replace(current_ref,"Jo L", "Jo L†"),ifelse( pubs.year$correspondingauthor[i] == "no" & pubs.year$cofirstauthor[i] == "yes",str_replace(current_ref,"Jo L", "Jo L§"),ifelse( pubs.year$correspondingauthor[i] == "yes" & pubs.year$cofirstauthor[i] == "yes",str_replace(current_ref,"Jo L", "Jo L†,§"), str_replace(current_ref,"Jo L", "Jo L")))) corr_label <- if(pubs.year$correspondingauthor[i] == "yes") " (†Corresponding Author)" else "" coauthor_label <- if(pubs.year$cofirstauthor[i] == "yes") " (§Co-first author)" else "" doi.link <- paste0( '', 'http://doi.org/', pubs.year$doi[i], '' ) # 4. Print the formatted lines cat(str_glue('
  • {bold_ref} {doi.link}{corr_label}{coauthor_label}

')) } } ```
